title: lepidium virginicum subsp. texanum
intro: Lepidium virginicum subsp. texanum, also known as Texas peppergrass, is a subspecies of the perennial herb peppercress. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health, due to its rich content of vitamins and minerals like vitamin C, iron, and calcium. Traditionally, Texas peppergrass has been prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and can also be added to food as a spice or used in herbal infusions to reap its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
